column2: EPIC Poison Thanos Enthrones the Barons of Battleworld
column27: 1st round was terrible. 2nd round got crazy lucky and pulled 2 silk uncommons early and was able to combo well with gamora to have a low turn count. lots of fun when you get lucky like that.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Not enough ways to get vp in this game. Thankfully stepford cuckoo made it so I could fight symbiotic armor before it bonded. That, 1 lord shingen, and magma men were the only villains worth vp I saw all game. Silk drawing and gamora's trigger artifact were awesome.
Never felt like my heroes clicked the way I wanted them to, but they still got the job done. A few early escapes when I got too many wounds, otherwise not too bad.
Quite challenging and tough to contain escapes.

One would think that loading up on Joe Fixit cards would give enough recruit to hit Network Nightmare, nope. Was able to keep the city in check but the game dragged trying to either wait for a master strike or enough recruit to hit Modok. I gave up and just loaded up on officers and finished the job really late in the game. Captain marvel and Rick Jones worked as well as i hopped, really fun setup.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: This was fun but challenging. Gladiator hulk's transformed card worked great in combining with photon and joe fix it (discarding but pulling the hulk card back in). It was challenging fighting modok in his transformation to “recruitment attack” and we barely won just as the frost giants were making a stampede at the end! Pretty epic ending!

Weird games. In my first run, I was in constant scramble mode and could barely keep ahead of anything, but edged out a victory with only a few cards left in the villain deck. For the second game, I was in constant control at about turn 20. Unfortunately, I over optimized my decks and could never land enough recruit to put the finishing blow on the transformed MODOK once all the Master Strikes had been played. I just let it slip away.
Not enough deck slimming and too many wounds. I was only able to hit MODOK once
I hate MODOK. I don’t think I’ve ever beaten him and this game moved so fast with the clones and giants. Brutal.
For how many times I shuffled any deck this game, I sure had a knack for always making it the worst shuffle possible.
